# Health Helpers — Medicare Supplement Insurance Rate Comparison

## About
Health Helpers is a licensed Medicare Supplement (Medigap) insurance rate comparison service. We help seniors compare Plan G, Plan F, and Plan N rates from A-rated carriers to find lower premiums for the same federally standardized coverage. Our licensed agents operate nationwide.

## Key Facts
- Medicare Supplement plans are federally standardized — Plan G is Plan G regardless of carrier
- The only difference between carriers is the premium price
- Most policyholders save $100–$200/month by switching carriers
- Free rate comparison with no obligation
- Licensed agents available in all 50 states
- We compare rates from A+ and A-rated carriers including Aflac, Medico, Aetna, Cigna, Mutual of Omaha, and others

## Frequently Asked Questions

### Am I paying too much for Medicare Supplement insurance?
If you haven't compared rates in the past year, there's a good chance you're overpaying. Insurance carriers often gradually increase premiums on loyal customers while offering lower rates to new policyholders. Since all Medigap plans are federally standardized, you can switch to a carrier with lower rates and get the exact same coverage.

### Is Plan G the same no matter which company I buy from?
Yes. By federal law, all Medicare Supplement plans are standardized. Plan G from Aflac covers the exact same benefits as Plan G from any other carrier. The only difference is the price.

### Can I switch Medicare Supplement carriers without losing coverage?
Yes, you can switch Medigap carriers at any time. Outside of your Medigap Open Enrollment Period, you may need to answer health questions. Licensed agents can guide you through the process.

### Why do Medicare Supplement rates vary so much by carrier?
Each carrier uses different pricing methods (attained-age, issue-age, or community-rated), has different risk pools, and operates in different regions. Some carriers also use price optimization, gradually raising rates on long-term customers.

### What is the difference between Plan G, Plan F, and Plan N?
Plan F covers 100% of Medicare gaps (available only if Medicare-eligible before Jan 1, 2020). Plan G covers everything except the annual Part B deductible ($240). Plan N has lower premiums but includes small copays for some visits. All three let you see any doctor who accepts Medicare with no referrals.
